zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Broughton於1515年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德時，她的父親Sir John Broughton31歲，母親Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"26歲。她於1535年與Sir Richard Osborne在英國英格蘭肯特阿福德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1570年在英國肯特阿福德享年55歲，埋葬在英國肯特阿福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Broughton于1515年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德时，她的父亲Sir John Broughton31岁，母亲Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"26岁。她于1535年与Sir Richard Osborne在英国英格兰肯特阿福德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。她去世于1570年在英国肯特阿福德享年55岁，埋葬在英国肯特阿福德。

nameMeanings.name: Broughton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places in all parts of England called Broughton. The first element is variously Old English brōc ‘brook’, burg ‘fortress’, or beorg ‘hill, mound’; the second is in all cases Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Braughton
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Broughton  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Broughton was born in 1515, in Westmorland, England, her father, Sir John Broughton, was 31 and her mother, Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", was 26. She married Sir Richard Osborne in 1535, in Ashford, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died in 1570, in Ashford, Kent, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Ashford, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Broughton est née en 1515, à Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Broughton, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Osborne en 1535, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée en 1570, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Broughton 1515 in Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Broughton 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" 26. Sie hat 1535 in Ashford, Kent, England, United Kingdom Sir Richard Osborne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb 1570 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Ashford, Kent, England und wurde in Ashford, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Broughton è nata il nel 1515 nel Westmorland, England, sua padre, Sir John Broughton, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Osborne nel 1535, nel Ashford, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì nel 1570, nel Ashford, Kent, England, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Ashford, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Broughtonが1515年にイギリスウエストモーランドで生まれた時，父Sir John Broughtonは31歳，母Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"は26歳であった。1535年にイギリスイギリスケントアシュフォードでSir Richard Osborneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1570年にイギリスケントアシュフォードで55歳で死去し，イギリスケントアシュフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Broughton(이)가 1515년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Broughton(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1535년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 Sir Richard Osborne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1570년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Broughton nasceu em 1515, em Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Broughton, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Osborne em 1535, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1570, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Broughton родилась в 1515 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Broughton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Osborne в 1535 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла в 1570 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Broughton nació en 1515, en Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Broughton, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I", tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Osborne en 1535, en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió en 1570, en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra.
